words.def: to officially end a law, a system or an institution
words.example: This tax should be abolished.

words.def: the deliberate ending of a pregnancy at an early stage
words.example: to support/oppose abortion

words.def: the fact of somebody being away from a place where they are usually expected to be; the occasion or period of time when somebody is away
words.example: in somebody's absence, The decision was made in my absence (= while I was not there).

words.def: not in a place because of illness, etc.
words.example: He was absent from work for two weeks.

words.def: extremely silly; not logical and sensible
words.example: That uniform makes the guards look absurd.

words.def: a large quantity that is more than enough
words.example: The brochure promised beautiful walks with an abundance of wildlife.

words.def: the use of something in a way that is wrong or harmful
words.example: alcohol/drug abuse

words.def: a school or college for special training
words.example: She trained at the Royal Academy of Music.

words.def: to happen faster or earlier; to make something happen faster or earlier
words.example: Inflation continues to accelerate.

words.def: the act of accepting a gift, an invitation, an offer, etc.
words.example: Please confirm your acceptance of this offer in writing.

words.def: that can be reached, entered, used, seen, etc.
words.example: The remote desert area is accessible only by helicopter.

words.def: an impressive thing that is done or achieved after a lot of work
words.example: It was one of the President's greatest accomplishments.

words.def: according to a rule or the way that somebody says that something should be done
words.example: in accordance with legal requirements

words.def: in a way that is appropriate to what has been done or said in a particular situation
words.example: We have to discover his plans and act accordingly.

words.def: the fact of being responsible for your decisions or actions and expected to explain them when you are asked
words.example: proposals for greater police accountability

words.def: responsible for your decisions or actions and expected to explain them when you are asked
words.example: The state spends taxpayers’ money and should be held accountable.

words.def: to gradually get more and more of something over a period of time
words.example: I seem to have accumulated a lot of books.

words.def: the process of gradually increasing or getting more and more of something over a period of time
words.example: the accumulation of wealth

words.def: a statement saying that you think a person is guilty of doing something wrong or illegal; the fact of accusing somebody
words.example: I don't want to make an accusation until I have some proof.

words.def: a person who is on trial for committing a crime
words.example: The accused was found innocent.

words.def: that contains acid or has the essential characteristics of an acid; that has a pH of less than seven
words.example: Rye is tolerant of poor, acid soils.

words.def: the act of getting something, especially knowledge, a skill, etc.
words.example: theories of child language acquisition

words.def: a unit for measuring an area of land; 4 840 square yards or about 4 050 square metres
words.example: 3 000 acres of parkland

words.def: the act of making something such as a device or chemical process start working
words.example: The activation of several target genes results in two major effects.

words.def: a person who works to achieve political or social change, especially as a member of an organization with particular aims
words.example: human/civil/animal rights activists

words.def: very serious or severe
words.example: There is an acute shortage of water.

words.def: the action or process of changing something, or of being changed, to suit a new purpose or situation
words.example: the adaptation of buildings for military purposes

words.def: to stick to something
words.example: Once in the bloodstream, the bacteria adhere to the surface of the red cells.

words.def: next to something
words.example: The planes landed on adjacent runways.

words.def: a small change made to something in order to correct or improve it
words.example: I've made a few adjustments to the design.

words.def: to manage and organize the affairs of a company, an organization, a country, etc.
words.example: to administer a charity/fund/school

words.def: connected with organizing the work of a business or an institution
words.example: an administrative job/assistant/error

words.def: a person whose job is to manage and organize the public or business affairs of a company or an institution, or a person who works in an office dealing with records, accounts, etc.
words.example: Such organizational decisions are made by the hospital administrators.

words.def: the act of accepting somebody into an institution, organization, etc.; the right to enter a place or to join an institution or organization
words.example: Hospital admission is not necessary in most cases.

words.def: a young person who is developing from a child into an adult
words.example: adolescents between the ages of 13 and 18 and the problems they face

words.def: the act of adopting a child; the fact of being adopted
words.example: She put the baby up for adoption.

words.def: negative and unpleasant; not likely to produce a good result
words.example: adverse change/circumstances/weather conditions

words.def: a person who supports or speaks in favour of somebody or of a public plan or action
words.example: advocate for something/somebody, an advocate for hospital workers

words.def: connected with beauty and art and the understanding of beautiful things
words.example: the aesthetic appeal of the songs

words.def: the feeling of liking or loving somebody/something very much and caring about them
words.example: Children need lots of love and affection.

words.def: the situation that exists as a result of an important (and usually unpleasant) event, especially a war, an accident, etc.
words.example: in the aftermath of something, A lot of rebuilding took place in the aftermath of the war.

words.def: feelings of anger and hate that may result in threatening or violent behaviour
words.example: The research shows that computer games may cause aggression.

words.def: connected with the science or practice of farming
words.example: agricultural policy/land/production/development

words.def: a person who helps another person, especially a politician, in their job
words.example: White House aides

words.def: although
words.example: He finally agreed, albeit reluctantly, to help us.

words.def: able to think quickly; quick to notice things
words.example: Suddenly he found himself awake and fully alert.

words.def: strange and frightening; different from what you are used to
words.example: an alien environment

words.def: to arrange something in the correct position, or to be in the correct position, in relation to something else, especially in a straight line
words.example: Make sure the shelf is aligned with the top of the cupboard.

words.def: arrangement in a straight line
words.example: the alignment of the sun, moon and earth at a particular time

words.def: very similar
words.example: My sister and I do not look alike.

words.def: a public statement that is made without giving proof, accusing somebody of doing something that is wrong or illegal
words.example: to investigate/deny/withdraw an allegation

words.def: to state something as a fact but without giving proof
words.example: allege (that)…, The prosecution alleges (that) she was driving carelessly.

words.def: expressed as though something is a fact but without giving any proof
words.example: crimes allegedly committed during the war

words.def: an agreement between countries, political parties, etc. to work together in order to achieve something that they all want
words.example: to form/make an alliance

words.def: to give something officially to somebody/something for a particular purpose
words.example: allocate something (for something), A large sum has been allocated for buying new books for the library.

words.def: an amount of money, space, etc. that is given to somebody for a particular purpose
words.example: We have spent our entire allocation for the year.

words.def: an amount of money that is given to somebody regularly or for a particular purpose
words.example: an allowance of $20 a day

words.def: a country that has agreed to help and support another country, especially in case of a war
words.example: our European/NATO allies

words.def: a chemical element. Aluminium is a light, silver-grey metal used for making pans, etc.
words.example: aluminium saucepans/window frames

words.def: doing something for pleasure or interest, not as a job
words.example: an amateur photographer

words.def: an official who lives in a foreign country as the senior representative there of their own country
words.example: the British Ambassador to Italy/in Rome

words.def: to change a law, document, statement, etc. slightly in order to correct a mistake or to improve it
words.example: He asked to see the amended version.

words.def: a small change or improvement that is made to a document or proposed new law; the process of changing a document or proposed new law
words.example: to introduce/propose/table an amendment (= to suggest it)

words.def: in the middle of or during something, especially something that causes excitement or fear
words.example: He finished his speech amid tremendous applause.

words.def: a comparison of one thing with another thing that has similar features; a feature that is similar
words.example: analogy (between A and B), The teacher drew an analogy between the human heart and a pump.

words.def: a heavy metal object that is attached to a rope or chain and dropped over the side of a ship or boat to keep it in one place
words.example: to drop anchor

words.def: a spirit who is believed to be a servant of God, and is sent by God to deliver a message or perform a task. Angels are often shown dressed in white, with wings.
words.example: a host of angels

words.def: with a name that is not known or that is not made public
words.example: an anonymous donor

words.def: the tools or other pieces of equipment that are needed for a particular activity or task
words.example: a piece of laboratory apparatus

words.def: attractive or interesting
words.example: Spending the holidays in Britain wasn't a prospect that I found particularly appealing.

words.def: physical desire for food
words.example: He suffered from headaches and loss of appetite.

words.def: to show your approval of somebody/something by clapping your hands (= hitting your open hands together several times)
words.example: He started to applaud and the others joined in.

words.def: that can be said to be true in the case of somebody/something
words.example: Give details of children where applicable (= if you have any).

words.def: to choose somebody for a job or position of responsibility
words.example: appoint somebody, They have appointed a new head teacher at my son's school.

words.def: pleasure that you have when you recognize and enjoy the good qualities of somebody/something
words.example: She shows little appreciation of good music.

words.def: not seeming to be based on a reason, system or plan and sometimes seeming unfair
words.example: The choice of players for the team seemed completely arbitrary.

words.def: relating to architecture and buildings
words.example: architectural features

words.def: a collection of historical documents or records of a government, a family, a place or an organization; the place where these records are stored
words.example: the National Sound Archive

words.def: a place with a flat open area in the middle and seats around it where people can watch sports and entertainment
words.example: a concert at Wembley Arena

words.def: used (often before a comparative or superlative adjective) when you are stating an opinion that you believe you could give reasons to support
words.example: He is arguably the best actor of his generation.

words.def: either of the two long parts that stick out from the top of the body and connect the shoulders to the hands
words.example: He escaped with only a broken arm.

words.def: a group or collection of things or people, often one that is large or impressive
words.example: a vast array of bottles of different shapes and sizes

words.def: to express or explain your thoughts or feelings clearly in words
words.example: She struggled to articulate her thoughts.

words.def: the grey or black powder that is left after something, especially tobacco, wood or coal, has burnt
words.example: cigarette ash

words.def: a strong desire to have or do something
words.example: I didn't realize you had political aspirations.

words.def: to have a strong desire to achieve or to become something
words.example: aspire (to something), She aspired to a scientific career.

words.def: the murder of an important or famous person, especially for political reasons
words.example: The president survived a number of assassination attempts.

words.def: the crime of attacking somebody physically
words.example: Both men were charged with assault.

words.def: to come together as a group; to bring people or things together as a group
words.example: All the students were asked to assemble in the main hall.

words.def: a group of people who have been elected to meet together regularly and make decisions or laws for a particular region or country
words.example: state/legislative/federal/local assemblies

words.def: to state clearly and definitely that something is true
words.example: assert that…, She continued to assert that she was innocent.

words.def: a statement saying that you strongly believe something to be true
words.example: He was correct in his assertion that the minister had been lying.

words.def: a statement that something will certainly be true or will certainly happen, particularly when there has been doubt about it
words.example: They called for assurances that the government is committed to its education policy.

words.def: protection that a government gives to people who have left their own country, usually because they were in danger for political reasons
words.example: to seek/apply for/be granted asylum

words.def: a cruel and violent act, especially in a war
words.example: In the war, both sides committed atrocities.

words.def: to succeed in getting something, usually after a lot of effort
words.example: Most of our students attained five ‘A’ grades in their exams.

words.def: the act of being present at a place, for example at school
words.example: Attendance at these lectures is not compulsory.

words.def: a lawyer, especially one who can act for somebody in court
words.example: The prosecuting attorney began with a short opening statement.

words.def: a quality or feature of somebody/something
words.example: Patience is one of the most important attributes in a teacher.

words.def: an official examination of business and financial records to see that they are true and correct
words.example: an annual audit

words.def: known to be real and what somebody claims it is and not a copy
words.example: I don't know if the painting is authentic.

words.def: to give official permission for something, or for somebody to do something
words.example: authorize something, I can authorize payments up to £5 000.

words.def: a car
words.example: the auto industry

words.def: the freedom for a country, a region or an organization to govern itself independently
words.example: a campaign in Wales for greater autonomy

words.def: the fact that something is possible to get, buy or find
words.example: the availability of cheap flights

words.def: to wait for somebody/something
words.example: He is in custody awaiting trial.

words.def: everything that can be seen around an event or scene
words.example: The mountains provided a dramatic backdrop for our picnic.

words.def: help and support from somebody to do something; financial support for something
words.example: The police gave the proposals their full backing.

words.def: extra help or support that you can get if necessary
words.example: The police had backup from the army.

words.def: money that somebody agrees to pay if a person accused of a crime does not appear at their trial. When bail has been arranged, the accused person is allowed to go free until the trial.
words.example: Can anyone put up bail for you?

words.def: the system of voting in writing and usually in secret; an occasion on which a vote is held
words.example: The chairperson is chosen by secret ballot.

words.def: a long piece of cloth with a message on it that is carried between two poles or hung in a public place to show support for something
words.example: A huge banner over the street said ‘Welcome home’.

words.def: not covered by any clothes
words.example: She likes to walk around in bare feet.

words.def: a large round container, usually made of wood or metal, with flat ends and, usually, curved sides
words.example: a beer/wine barrel

words.def: the lowest tone or part in music, for instruments or voices
words.example: He always plays his stereo with the bass turned right up.

words.def: a piece of wood with a handle, made in various shapes and sizes, and used for hitting the ball in games such as baseball, cricket and table tennis
words.example: a baseball/cricket bat

words.def: a place where a battle is being fought or has been fought
words.example: heavy casualties on the battlefield

words.def: a part of the sea, or of a large lake, partly surrounded by a wide curve of the land
words.example: the Bay of Bengal

words.def: a line of light, electromagnetic waves or particles
words.example: narrow beams of light/sunlight

words.def: an animal, especially one that is large or dangerous, or one that is unusual
words.example: wild/savage/ferocious beasts
